reviews.review_description:
No 3D movies at launch is a major disappointment.  I didn’t expect the whole catalogue.   But I expected 2 or 3.  The fact that these headsets have been around for so long and there is NO native way to watch 3D moves on it is completely ridiculous.  There used to be like 12 of them on Big Screen, but I don’t think those are there anymore either.  I don’t blame Disney.  I blame Meta.  Sinking millions ￼into freaking metaverse horizon worlds instead of cutting actual deals with actual studios to get actual 3D content onto their 3D devices.  It boggles the mind.
 
Even the Avatar Fire and Ash preview is 2D.  You can watch the 3D one on freaking YouTube.  But not on their own app.

“3D capabilities are coming to Disney+ on Meta Quest in the future”
Capabilities? We know this is about business monopoly negotiations, not some technical conundrum you’re wrestling with.

I'll be a little more generous than most reviewers and give this app a 4, it bugs me that if it gets minimized and then resume it's back to the home screen instead of playing the video or pausing it, though would prefer if it kept playing. Other then that can't think of anything else other than no 3D movies available which is the biggest boneheaded mistake Disney and Meta could have made to give the middle finger to millions of quest owns and cater to a couple of ten of thousands of vision pro users. Just give us a timeline of when they'll become available. Treat your customer base a little better. Bring the 3D  movies now.

When I click on the option to watch a movie in full screen the little highlight bar below the movie that says Disney and shows the little dots always stays there and it's distracting.  It doesn't need to be there. It ruin the experience and it adds extra light to a perfectly dark background that I would like to have please fix 🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻

The quality of the video is better than I was expecting: it’s much clearer than on BigScreen for example. The lack of themed screens and, in particular, 3D is very disappointing. Hopefully that’s in the pipe. The fact that neither Disney or Meta is addressing that particular elephant in the room is a slight worry though.

Don’t get me wrong I love Disney, but I find it disgraceful that they seem to have given Apple exclusive rights to their 3d catalog. Especially since the number of consumers that actually own Apple Vision  Pros is ridiculously small compared to the amount of Meta Oculus headsets. Seems like there is a LOT of demand in these reviews for Disney to make their 3D content available here…and I wholeheartedly agree! Come on Disney and Meta this would be a great public relations move as these rating and reviews have made abundantly clear. This app would likely drive sales for headsets too. It’s still surprising to me that 3D Movie content is basically nowhere to be found on these headsets platforms and apps…seems like a common sense move to me.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your feedback with us. While 3D capabilities are coming to Disney+ on Meta Quest in the future, we don't have any updates to share at this time. We'll be sure to pass along your interest. Let us know if there's anything else we can assist you with!

reviews.review_description: There's a glitch that hasn't been adressed by anyone yet. When watching any kind of video (especially on full screen viewer) the screen lock control button is still viewable in the top right corner. I've deleted the app & re logged in but that didn't fix the problem. Please fix this because it's very distracting to the VR experience!
I have updated headset, deleted and reinstalled the app and I'm still getting an error message saying it cant connect to wifi and then the app closes on it's own. Typical  Disney  L.

These days, you have to be grateful if a streaming service even offers a dedicated app for the Quest. This one is good, the picture quality is very decent. Dolby Vision and HDR are also supported, which is great. 

More options for adjusting the image size would be desirable, but presumably they want to avoid any loss of quality if the user selects an overly large image. A virtual cinema would also be nice, other apps offer something like that and I think the Prime app had it back in the day. 

3D content will surely arrive when Avatar 3 launches on Disney+. Otherwise, James Cameron's collaboration with Meta Quest wouldn't make much sense, so I'll just wait and see.

I have a supervised Meta profile for a 10 years old and Disney + is allowed in apps, I can launch it in the kid profile, I have the option to log in but after the app says that there is no eligible profile to run the app. I have the option to log out... In my adult profile Disney + works as intended and I can switch the watching profiles, including the kid one.
this is very frustrating and annoying and is part of the Meta "security" for kids profile... please fix this or provide a bypass solution.

Not a patch on Spatial TV, which was developed by a tiny dev. No interactive lighting, no easy placement of screen, no environments, no 3D.

That this, low effort, nothing of an app, comes from Disney, a company with more money than God, tells you everything you need to know about VR support right now.

Calling this an app, is an overstatement. It's more like a browser extention, not even a good one.

People are complaining about the lack of 3D movies, but I downloaded the Disney Plus app to watch Disney Plus content, which is what I am doing right now. The app works great and is definitely better than using the website. It even lets you download content for offline use. The fact that people are complaining about such a small thing is completely crazy. I know watching movies in 3D can make them feel more impressive, but they are the same movies at the end of the day, with or without 3D visuals.

Also, the app does NOT claim to have 3D content, as the description of the app does not say anything about it. The 3D titles inside the app were added a while after the Vision Pro app released, and it stands to reason that this app will also support 3D content later on. 
The app does not deserve a bad rating for not having something they never claimed to have in the first place. People need to learn to be patient and not expect more than they are promised.
O don’t care if it’s better than the browser but worse than any other streaming app and doesn’t have 3D Movies. Trash.

This app is a little bit better than using the browser but not by much. Its just so bare bones, it does nothing to take advantage of the possibilities that VR offers us for entertainment. Imagine for example watching Finding Nemo in an underwater environment and the characters swim around while you're watching the movie, or The Lion King in a savannah with the animals walking around. Or you could put Disney Parks rides on here and then we could ride them in 360 VR or, how incredible would it be to watch Happily Ever After fireworks show in VR? I'd even pay a bit extra for that. Instead we just got the mobile app on a big floating flat screen.
I’m in the Middle East and it doesn’t support my region

reviews.review_title: Pathetic with as much money as Disney's
reviews.review_description:
With as much money as Disney's got and they put this kind of junk out. 
My biggest complaint is easily you move your headset a little bit most  videos sites that you watch in the browser such as Tubi, Paramount, Pluto etc keep playing if you move your headset up for a second to see something no problem. Then the other thing is even if it does stop you're still on the same screen that you were before. Not this freaking app ! it takes you back to the main screen and you have to select the thing that you want to watch again from the list. Just amazingly stupid user interface programming.

Streaming seems low bit-rate 1080, but download ability is a big plus and quality seems better.  Still needs 4K quality for downloads and 3D Disney library (this is a VR app).  Viewing environment is good, app worked good. Controllers dim after a few seconds.

reviews.review_title: App Not Working Despite Active Subsc
reviews.review_description: I’m in the Middle East, and the app isn’t working even though I have an active Disney+ subscription.
I love this app in vr watched all the new alien earth my god i would recommend

reviews.review_title: App’s great, but leaves much to desire.
reviews.review_description:
So unlike a lot of people here, I’m not going to complain about the lack of 3D content. Not only do I understand that it’s probably a licensing issue rather than a lack of effort by the app developers, but they are stating in responses that the app will receive 3D content in the undisclosed future (based on the 3D fire and ash trailer I’m hoping that means roughly when fire and ash comes to Disney+). Instead, I want to point out a few features that could potentially be added in the meantime while waiting for Vision Pro’s feature exclusivity to expire.

1) Spatial Lighting- simply adding spatial lighting around the video itself can make the video feel much more immersive even without immersive environments.

2) Alternate Immersive Environments- I don’t know how extensive the exclusivity deal is for Apple, but if the deal is only for the environments you already made, maybe make some new ones? If not full environments, maybe just use mixed reality and allow a person to add props to their room that react to the movie, like how the sphero BB-8 would react to Star Wars movies. That could add to the immersive storytelling aspect without causing issues with whatever deal you made with Apple.

3) Add a few mixed reality interactive features- again, don’t know how much you can do with this considering Apple already has the Main Street Electrical Parade, but maybe add some mini games like a firework show designer where people can pick music from Disney movies and have different types of fireworks they can set off at specific times and places in mixed reality.

4) Professionally made 360 and 180 park videos- this could be used by guests to better understand what they are getting into when it comes to certain rides or maybe just provide some peaceful views of pavilions in the parks. I can’t emphasize enough the importance of 180° videos for ride-throughs. Nobody needs to see the back of a doom buggy so put those pixels where they count. There are several already made on YouTube but those have the entire environment full of guests and all the ride-through are super low quality because half the pixels are wasted on the back of the ride.

Essentially I think really anything to make it feel less like a 2D app would be very welcome to the audience, really just take advantage of the limitless possibilities of VR and make whatever feels like it’s worth making.

Other than that, the app is already pretty great. Sound is great and the picture quality doesn’t leave much to complain about.

They shouldn't be listing anything as being Dolby Vision as the Quest can't even display HDR content, never mind Dolby Vision.  Video resolution isn't even 4k, but quality is ok.  Absolutely no replacement for a decent TV quality wise. The app is very bare bones, but better than using the browser.

And of course it should have 3D content. That would be the only reason for me to use this app.

I was all set to subscribe but thanks to all those who alerted no 3D for Meta Quest, I'll just wait until 3D is added before spending my money for movies I've seen already in flatworld.
There is a preview of some, like Avatar in 3D in the DEOVR app that looks amazing.

The app doesn't come with its own environments, and yes, there is room for improvement in that regard.

However, I'm still a happy customer because the experience is pretty nice. I'm using the "Midnight" scenery for "Immersive Home". Then go into the focus view. The background dimming can be adjusted as an option. The idea here is to get the background as dark as possible.

This results in a large curved screen (can be flat) which takes up most of your vision, and there is nothing distracting in the background. 

There is a good ability to move the screen around including forwards and backwards to a degree. You can watch while reclining no problem.

I still prefer the "void" environment with the 4XVR player, and I wish it had something like that, but with a little experimentation the screen and viewing experience is already excellent.

I'm not going to give one star just because the app doesn't have 3D movies at launch; although across the industry there should be more support for the 3D format including with physical releases. I'm sorry, but it's criminal that Disney didn't release Tron: Ares on 3D Blu Ray.

Minor issue: after adjusting the background dimming, it seems like you need to restart the device, or the bar below the screen will not fade out as it should, when not needed.

It may also help the viewing experience to turn off hands, under settings / accessibility / mobility.

reviews.review_title: A good app, but could use more
reviews.review_description: Its a perfectly good app for watching Disney+. I dont know why people are review bombing it because theres no 3D content. They never said there would be any. Disney+ has an exclusivity contract with the Apple Vision Pro, so they cant release 3D content on the Quest yet. I would appreciate some 3D environments for watching in though. Instead of just passthrough.
No 3D so canclled

reviews.review_description: I love Disney+ but I hope they add 3D movies like the Apple Vision Pro app. Meta needs to support their codec or Disney needs to find an alternative!

developer_response.body: Hey there! While 3D capabilities are coming to Disney+ on Meta Quest in the future, we don't have any updates to share at this time. We'll be sure to pass along your interest. Let us know if there's anything else we can assist you with.

reviews.review_description:
Image quality is pretty decent for the most part. Resolution is great, but looks more washed out than it should be though compared to other video apps. Could do with some brightness / contrast settings to fine tune things.

As others have said, missing 3D content which is a main draw for VR users. Judging by comments on here from app developers,  it's on the way, which is great news. 

Will give it 3 stars for a good starting point, but with some extra additions, this will be a must have app and subscription  for me.

While no commercials launch, you can see on the on the time bar where they are scheduled to occur on smart TVs and streaming boxes. However in the Quest app, it appears when the show gets to these points it cuts out way before, missing significant content. I'm binging Andor right now and these breaks are skipping important bits of the story line. I can tell when at the start of the next episode, at the recap, segments from the preceding episode are played back that absolutely did not show. This is a bit frustrating. Disney can do better!
Where is 3d i what 3d movies

developer_response.body: Hey there! While 3D capabilities are coming to Disney+ on Meta Quest in the future, we don't have any updates to share at this time. We'll be sure to pass along your interest. Let us know if there's anything else we can assist you with!

reviews.review_description:
Finally!  I’ve been waiting for this one.

Still needs: 4k and 3D support.

Also, when I view a movie and it’s expanded it doesn’t blacken out my room.  I turned the dimming all the way down but as soon as I turn my head it turns off the dimming again.  Maybe I’m doing something wrong?  I don’t know…
It doesn’t seem to provide 3D titles. But on certain movies, it does show 3D as a supported format in the details page. So I hope it comes soon!
Bad experience, low resolution, No 3D movies, useless….very disappointed

developer_response.body: We're sorry to hear about this experience. While 3D capabilities are coming to Disney+ on Meta Quest in the future, we don't have any updates to share at this time. We'll be sure to pass along your interest. Let us know if there's anything else we can assist you with!

reviews.review_title: Great app - No low battery warnings
reviews.review_description: While watching in full screen, there are no low battery warnings one quest 3. None. All settings are set correctly as I get the warnings in other apps. The only major issue with this app I've encountered. Otherwise, great app

I was looking forward to enjoy a dedicated Disney+ App on Meta and it's finally here. I admit that I was hoping for it to launch with some 3D content, however, Disney has an exclusive deal with Apple for the time being so we will have to wait for the content to be added in the future. Other than this, the App runs perfect like on my other devices. 10/10

Despite the bad reviews because of the lack of 3D films, this is the only way to watch 4k movies HDR and I love it and what better place to experience to watch IMAX movies than on VR, the only remaining wish is meta to have a 4.43:1 IMAX cinema environment. Keep the good work
Only a handful of IMAX enhanced and no 3D at all. That’s a little disappointing. Not everyone can afford Vision Pro. Disney caters to the wealthy.
They have they just don't want to put them on really annoying because Vr has the capability even if it was only a few to try it out and then pay to see more or something

I have been waiting for this native Disney app. It is a full treatment of the flat screen app. 
The resolution is consistent no matter you expand the screen to full size or have a personalized sized screen. 3D is not available (yet).
I don't have the account level to download content but it is available for off-line viewing.
Well done.

Honestly this is one the best ones I’ve seen, Netflix was abysmal. Great streaming quality and I can dock it in my virtual home and it starts right up everytime.
Only downside is the lack of volume control and where is the access to your 3D library
Other than that you did a really good job in comparison with your competitors 👍

Solid viewing experience. Large screen. Option the curve the view to make it a little more immersive. It will be 5* if/when 3D is added. 

Looking hopefully to the future with immersive viewing environments and 3D content. I’m ready for the return of 3D content.
Colors are washed up after losing Dolby Vision. Please fix that.
No reason that they dont have 3d, lazy devs
You cowards!! The quality of the video streaming is great. No buffering or stuttering. But give us 3D movies!! You have more than enough!!
